title: A simplified authority seal: a thin ring boundary and the `->` operator at center. The ring is the complete jurisdictional boundary of the governed domain; the operator is what the seal certifies. Unlike 44-sealed-contract, there is no decorative complexity — just the boundary and the certified law.
conceptualBridge: The ring seal means Precept's definition is the complete governing boundary because the circle marks where jurisdiction begins and ends, and the `->` at center is what the contract certifies: every declared transition is structurally valid within it.
decision: Kept at threshold (4.0): the circular boundary adds a jurisdictional dimension to the `->` operator, but the thin outline ring reduces structural weight; if promoted, would compete with 39-arrow-code as the code-aesthetic representative — only one can stay unless both justify the slot.
